text.title: Diff-Coverage Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns that hollow out diff-coverage adoption. Each ties a symptom to a root cause and a concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: PR comment moves diff coverage to a footnote; whole-repo coverage becomes the headline number again.
antipattern.root-cause: Whole-repo number is more flattering when diff coverage is uncomfortable; team reverts to the metric that looks better.
antipattern.fix: Lint the PR-comment template: headline_metric must be diff_coverage_percent. Testing lead owns the template; changes require approval.

antipattern.symptom: coverage-diff.yml exclusion list grows to dozens of paths within a year; the metric covers a shrinking fraction of the repo.
antipattern.root-cause: Exclusions are the path of least resistance for coverage-allergic teams; quarterly cleanup never happens.
antipattern.fix: Each exclusion requires a `reason` field of at least 10 characters and reviewer approval. Quarterly cleanup PR challenges entries older than 6 months.

antipattern.symptom: Aggregate diff coverage passes the 80% gate; one file with 30% diff coverage slips through.
antipattern.root-cause: Aggregate threshold is met by a high-coverage helper file masking a low-coverage business-logic file.
antipattern.fix: Per-file gate: every file in the diff must hit its threshold individually. merge_gate_verdict is false if any per_file row fails.

antipattern.symptom: Diff-coverage adoption rejects most PRs because the codebase has near-zero baseline coverage.
antipattern.root-cause: Tooling lacks the tests it would gate against; the gate fires on the absence of upstream investment.
antipattern.fix: Skip this methodology while baseline is zero. Invest in baseline coverage first; then introduce the gate at a low default_threshold and ramp up.

antipattern.symptom: Dashboard exists but is never reviewed; low-coverage files trend down silently.
antipattern.root-cause: No standing review converts the trend into an action; the dashboard becomes wall art.
antipattern.fix: Monthly testing meeting reviews the dashboard. Specific actions per low-coverage file: investment, exclusion (with reason), or refactor.

antipattern.symptom: Same threshold applied to billing code and to throwaway scripts; either billing is under-tested or scripts block merges.
antipattern.root-cause: Uniform thresholds ignore that risk varies by area; the gate becomes either too soft or too brittle.
antipattern.fix: Define per-glob overrides: high (≥90) for money/auth/security, default for product code, low or zero for ops scripts with documented reason.
